segment-text: Chris Rock & Aeysha Carr Based on "Death at a Funeral" by Dean Craig MUSIC CUE: "DON'T WORRY, BE HAPPY" by Bobby McFerrin plays as we
INT. FUNERAL HOME - DAY
We pan across a room filled with caskets.
EXT. CHURCH - DAY
People somberly walk into a church.
EXT. FUNERAL HOME - DAY
We see the signs outside of various funeral homes.
EXT. CEMETERY - DAY
We close in on a HEADSTONE that reads: "DEATH AT A FUNERAL".
EXT. CHURCH - DAY
ASIAN PALL BEARERS carry a coffin into a church.
EXT. CEMETERY - DAY

We push in on another headstone that reads: "Starring Chris Rock". As we push through the cemetery, we see various co- stars' names chiseled on headstones. We see a fleet of hearses driving through a cemetery.
INT. FLORIST - DAY
We see a FLORIST making a funeral arrangement.
EXT. CEMETERY - DAY
CLOSE UP of another headstone that has the director's name chiseled on it.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

MUSIC CUE: The music fades out. TIGHT SHOT on AARON (CHRIS ROCK) thirties, dressed in a black suit and tie. He looks straight ahead with a somber expression. The camera pulls back to reveal he is watching FOUR UNDERTAKERS (INCLUDING BRIAN) place a coffin on a raised platform. The living room has been prepared for the funeral service, with several rows of chairs laid out.
Would you like to see...

Aaron nods his head "yes", as BRIAN begins to open the coffin. Aaron crosses to the coffin. There's a dramatic moment as Aaron moves to the coffin and braces himself for what he's about to see. The coffin opens and Aaron stares long and hard at the body.
Brian... who is that?
Excuse me?
Who is this man I'm looking at? 'Cause it's not my father.
Are you sure?
Are you asking me if I'm sure I know what my father looks like?
People make mistakes in times of grief.
Man, look at the damn body!
Brian peers into the coffin. We see it's an ASIAN MAN.
Oh shit!
The other undertakers look embarrassed.
Come on, Brian. You know what my father looks like. He used to take you to little league.
I'm sorry, man. I got a little nervous. This almost never happens.
It's happened before?! You mean this is a normal thing?... This isn't Burger King. You can't just mess up my order.
I think I know where he is.
You think?
INT. ASIAN FUNERAL - SAME TIME
Close up of the casket opening. People look confused. The MAN looking into the coffin speaks Chinese with subtitles. ASIAN MAN
INT. LIVING ROOM - SAME TIME
I better .,see my dad in this coffin in an hour, or you're going to take his place.
The undertakers pick up the coffin and shuffle out awkwardly.
Don't worry. I'll get him, man.
You do that. (TO HIMSELF) My people, my people, my people...
INT. BEDROOM - DAY
Aaron enters the bedroom. His wife, MICHELLE, also thirties, attractive, is putting on makeup with a thermometer hanging from her mouth. Aaron stands looking dumbfounded.
They brought the wrong body. Can you believe that?
(muffled because of
The wrong body?! Do they know where he is?
They think so. They're getting him now.
The thermometer beeps and Michelle removes it from her mouth..
I told you not to use Marshals. But no, you wanted to keep it in the neighborhood. I know Swartz costs a little more, but at least they know where the bodies are.
Michelle writes something down on a piece of paper as Aaron crosses the room and sits on the bed.
You were right. Please don't rub it in, not today. I've got so much to worry about.
Michelle gets up and heads to the bathroom as Aaron takes out some index cards and looks them over.
Dad insisted on having the funeral here, Mom's crying all the time, Ryan didn't help with anything, not like he ever does! Plus my eulogy sucks. I can't wait until this day is over.
Oh come on, honey. I'm sure it's not that bad.
If it is, at least Dad's too dead to hear it.
Michelle exits the bathroom.
Honey, it's going to be fine.
It doesn't matter what I write. All anybody's going to be thinking is "why isn't Ryan doing the eulogy? Even I'm going to be thinking it.
She holds him close.
Baby, you're the oldest. You have to do the eulogy. Besides, I think you're just as good a writer as your brother. You just need to let someone read your book.
Aaronlooks away.
Soon. It's not ready to be read yet.
I'm sure it's fine. Once the funeral's over, and everything dies
Aaron gives her a look.
Sorry. Look, once this is all over, you know your mom's thinking about selling the house, so you know we've got to get out.of here.
Aaron looks a little uncomfortable and heads to the bathroom.
You called them, right?
Silence from Aaron in the next room.
Aaron?... You put the down payment on the condo, right?
I said I'd take care of it and I'll take care of it.
Michelle looks frustrated and crosses into the bathroom.
INT. BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
Aaron is at the mirror.

You better, because I can't live with your mother anymore. I've done my time, now it's time for your brother to take over. From now on, it's only Christmas and birthdays for us. Once we move, we can concentrate on your writing and our baby.
Michelle gets down on her knees and starts unzipping Aaron's pants. Aaron pulls back.
Hey, hey, hey. Are you serious? We have to do this today? Michelle is still on the ground.
Yes. Today's the last day of this cycle.
Can I at least find the body first?
EXT. FREEWAY - WIDE SHOT - DAY
We see NORMAN'S CAR speeding along the freeway.
INT. NORMAN'S CAR - MOVING - DAY
NORMAN, black, slightly heavier and riddled with anxiety, is driving. His friend, DEREK, an attractive, white, clean cut doctor, rides in the passenger seat.
I hate funerals. It's all death, death, death.
I don't think funerals are designed for anybody to really like.
I don't want a regular funeral. When I die, I want people to celebrate my life.
Not me. I want it to be the saddest day ever. I want the stock market to stop. Schools to close. And no garbage pick-up.
Derek starts to light a cigarette.
You still smoke?
You used to smoke.
I used to piss my pants too, but I stopped. The only thing I want black is my skin. At least open the window.
Derek opens the window very slightly.
I've got enough problems of my own to deal with.
(DUBIOUS) Yeah? What problems?
I told you... About the rash...
Got to use condoms.
.on my hand...
Still got to use condoms.
Look at it.
Norman pulls up his sleeve and makes Derek look at his wrist, but he doesn't take a great deal of interest.
That's not a rash.
What is it?
I don't know, but it doesn't look right.
Oh, damn.
Norman continues to drive. Derek continues to smoke.
If you hate funerals so much why are you coming?
You know why.
Come on, man, leave Elaine alone.
I can't. She's the one.
So you're going to win her back over a dead body?
If I have to.
INT. ELAINE'S CAR - MOVING - DAY
ELAINE, very attractive, 27, and her boyfriend OSCAR, white, 35, are driving along wearing funeral attire. Oscar looks quite anxious as he keeps his eyes firmly on the road.
Are you okay?
Yeah, just a little nervous...
About seeing my dad?
I just don't think I've ever been so openly hated by anyone before.
He doesn't hate you. He just loved Derek.
Great. That makes me feel much better.
At least you know it's not you, so try not to get so nervous when he's around.
I can't help it. He puts me on edge. I feel like I owe him money.
He's going to be more.concerned that his brother-in-law just died. He won't be focused on you. Anyway, when I tell him we're getting married, he'll have to accept you.

Elaine takes his hand. As they exchange a loving look, Oscar takes his eyes off the road for just a brief moment. Suddenly there's a BUMP!!! And we see a bicyclist flying over Oscar's car. Oscar screeches to a halt. They jump out of the car.
Oh my God. Are you okay?
The guy gets up. He's fine.
Yeah, I'm fine.
Are you sure? Is anything broken? I'm so sorry.
It was my fault. I ran a light.
Oscar starts hyperventilating.
I could have killed you. What if you had cracked your head open? I'd have blood on my hands!
Elaine shakes him.
Oscar, calm down! (BEAT) (MORE)
You heard the man, he ran a light. Come on, let's get out of here before he wants to sue.
She grabs him and pulls him into the car.
INT. BATHROOM - DAY
The camera is close on Michelle's head which is moving back and forth. We pull back to reveal Aaron. They are doing it standing up.
What's taking you so long?
I just started.
You want this baby as much me. Come on.
I'm trying.
If I didn't want you to come, you would have done it five minutes ago.
Do I have to do this today?
The door opens and a HOUSEKEEPER sticks her head in.
Who's picking up Uncle Russell?--
Aaron and Elaine quickly pull apart. As the Housekeeper steps back out into the hall.
Oh shit, Uncle Russell.
Aaron fixes his pants as he exits the room.
INT. NORMAN'S CAR MOVING - DAY
Norman's cell phone RINGS. He answers.
Hello?... How are you?... Okay..
INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
I need you to pick up Uncle Russell.
INT. NORMAN'S CAR - MOVING - CONTINUOUS
Uncle Russell?! Damn! He's always in a bad mood.
INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
It's not a mood if he's always in it. It's his personality. Can you do it?
INT. NORMAN'S CAR - MOVING - CONTINUOUS
Alright... 'Alright. I'll do it. See you soon. Bye.
Norman hangs up irritated.
EXT. WIDE SHOT OF A FREEWAY - DAY
We see Norman's car exiting the freeway.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Aaron and Michelle are standing to the side as the undertakers place the coffin on the stand. Brian nervously lifts the lid and Aaron looks inside.
(RESPECTFUL) So is this your father?
Come on, man. You know that's my father.
Just trying to be professional. What up, Michelle?
(DRYLY) Brian.
He closes the lid and all four undertakers quietly head off. Aaron looks up and sees his mother, Cynthia, has entered the room. Cynthia is clearly drained and tired. She stares at the coffin.
Mom, how are you feeling?
There were so many things we wanted to do... We were going to go to China.
China? You don't even like Chinese food.

That's not the point. I haven't been feeling good. Who knows how long I'm going to live without him. It happens all the time. One spouse goes and then the next. I mean, it might be different if I had a grandchild to take my mind off of everything.
Cynthia, I-- They all cross to the coffin.
(cuts Michelle off) Come on, Mom. You're healthy. You'll be fine. You've got your friends and family...
Cynthia, Aaron and Michelle stand around the coffin.
Where is Ryan? Have you heard from him?
He'll be here soon.
Just seeing that boy's face makes me feel better. I've missed him so much. My baby.
She looks down at the coffin. Aaron turns away frustrated. Michelle gently touches the coffin.
Do you need anything, Cynthia?
Yeah, you can get your hand off my husband's coffin. You're leaving smudge marks.
Michelle tries to wipe it off with her sleeve.
EXT. STREET - JEFF'S HOUSE - DAY MUSIC: "PAPER PLANE" by M.I.A.
Elaine and Oscar are walking up the stairs of a typical college apartment building.
INT. JEFF'S HOUSE - SAME
The music is coming from Jeff's speakers. JEFF, dressed for the funeral, sits at his desk. He's holding a pill bottle that says: "VALIUM."

Yeah, I found them. The ones in the Valium bottle?... Yeah... I'll get them to you this evening... Can't do this afternoon... It's your fault you left them here... I've got a funeral... My uncle died... Yeah, tragic... No, I haven't taken any. I saw what they did to you... What are they anyway?...
The doorbell rings.
I've gotta go, my sister's here... I'll take them with me and stop by after the funeral... Bye.
The doorbell rings again.
Hang on!
Jeff stands up and we see that he's only half dressed for the funeral. He doesn't have any pants on. He puts the bottle on the counter then crosses to the door and lets Elaine and Oscar inside.
Hey, what's up?
Do you always open the door half naked? Come on, Jeff. We're going to be late.
I've just got to put my pants on and I'm ready.
Oscar is still a little shaky.
(RE: OSCAR) Is he okay?
Oscar's freaked out. A guy on a bike ran into the car.
It came out of nowhere. I didn't see him. I'm sorry.
It's okay, baby. (then to Jeff) Go put your damn pants on.
I'm going. I'm going.
Jeff heads off to the bathroom and closes the door. Elaine spots the bottle of Valium on the table and picks it up.
Here. Take this..: It'll calm you down.
What is it?
It's just Valium. (gets a glass of water) I used to take them all the time... It'll make you feel better, promise.
Are you sure? I mean, I've got to drive.
It'll be okay. I'll drive, honey. Come on, open wide.
She pops the pill in his mouth and hands him some water. Oscar swallows. Jeff returns wearing pants.
Ready. Let's do it.
Oscar and Elaine leave, Jeff surreptitiously picks up the Valium bottle, puts it in his pocket, then follows them out.
INT/EXT. ENTRY WAY - SAME TIME

CAMERA is in the entry way now shooting outside. A TOWN CAR exits, leaving Ryan in front of the house. RYAN, Aaron's younger brother is taller, more confident, and more successful. He moves slowly towards CAMERA and through the front doorway as CAMERA PULLS BACK with him.
Hello! Mom!
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Michelle, Aaron and Cynthia are on the couch as Ryan enters the living room.
Ryan!
Cynthia runs over to Ryan and flings her arms around him. Aaron looks a bit lost in the background with Michelle but they walk over.
I know this is hard, Mom. But you're going to be okay.
You always know exactly what to say, Ryan. I'm so glad you're here.
I'm always here for you, Mom.
I guess if people died every week, we'd see you all the time.
Ryan turns to Aaron and Michelle.
Aaron, how are you?
I'm alright.
How was the flight?

Okay. I had to buy the seat next to me because I don't trust the airlines with my bags. And there was a lot of turbulence... I guess that's the thing about flying first class. It doesn't matter how much you pay if the plane crashes, you still end up dead-- uh...
Ryan realizes what he's said and stops speaking abruptly. It's too much for Cynthia who rushes upstairs.
"End up dead"?
Oh damn.
I'll make sure she's okay.
Michelle heads off upstairs.
EXT. LAKEVIEW RETIREMENT COMMUNITY - SAME TIME
We see Norman's car turn into "Lakeview Retirement Community." He drives past the sign and through a gate.
INT. NORMAN'S CAR/EXT. LAKEVIEW - DAY
Norman is driving behind three OLD LADIES on mobile chairs. He's honking at them. He finally sees Uncle Russell.
There he is.
You didn't tell me the guy was in a wheelchair.
UNCLE RUSSELL, an elderly man in a wheelchair, sits in his chair. Norman drives forward and stops alongside the old man. He rolls down his window and speaks very slowly with a huge smile on his face.
(OVER ENUNCIATING) Hello, Uncle Russell. How are you? We're here to take you to the funeral.
Where the fuck have you been, you fat bastard? You're late. If you took any longer I'd be dead, too.
The fake smile falls off Norman's face.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Ryan sees the coffin and slowly heads over with Aaron trailing behind. He stands over the coffin. It finally registers that his DAD is actually in there.
It's hard to see Dad like this. But he's in a better place now.
When does dead beat sick?
I guess you're right. This is why I try not to get close to people. There's too much emotion involved.
Well that explains why you never call.
What do you want me to say? Some families talk to each other all the time, some just see each other at funerals. That's us.
I guess it is.
Ryan takes a seat in one of the chairs flanking the coffin. Aaron sits as well.
By the way, I'm going to need that money.
What money?
The money for all this... the funeral. You're supposed to pay half.
What about the insurance?
That's Mom's money.
What about the pension?
That's Mom's money.
Oh.
So we're spliting the cost.
Are we?
Yes. We are.
Oh, okay.
So?
I don't have it right now.
What do you mean, you don't have it? You just bought a first class seat for your bag.
And that's why I don't have any cash.
INT.. ELAINE'S CAR - MOVING - DAY
Elaine is now driving, Oscar is sitting in the passenger seat looking a little spaced out, with Jeff in the back. Jeff leans forward.
So, poor old Uncle Eddie...
Yeah. But he was sick for so long. He's much better off now.
Dead? Since when does dead beat sick?
Shut up, Jeff.
Jeff turns his attention to Oscar.
I love when you come to family functions, Oscar. Because then Dad focuses all his hate on you.
There's no response from Oscar. He looks distantly out the window. The pills are starting to kick in.
Oscar?
Was there a dog in here just now?
Elaine looks at Oscar very-strangely.
What?
He sniffs.
Dalmatian? No, Schnauzer... A beat.
What the hell are you talking about?
Oscar falls silent and just goes back to looking out the window.
INT. NORMAN'S CAR - LATER
Uncle Russell is asleep in the back. Norman is looking for a parking space, but the street and long driveway are packed with cars. A catering truck and flower van are taking up a lot of space.
There's nowhere to park.
Then the small flower delivery van pulls out. Derek points out the newly empty space.
That truck is pulling out. Park there.
Alright.
Just as he's about to drive in, another car takes it.
What the hell?
EXT. DRIVEWAY - CONTINUOUS
Norman gets out of the car. The other car contains Jeff, Oscar and Elaine. Jeff gets out first followed by Elaine.
Elaine, I was going to park there.
Are you family, Norman?
Well, no, but I'm like family.
No such thing.
Derek leans out the window.
Hi, Elaine.
What are you doing here?
Your father invited me.
Oh please.
She grabs Oscar and heads to the house followed by Jeff.
INT. NORMAN'S CAR - CONTINUOUS
Norman gets back into his car. Uncle Russell stirs.
What's happening?
Did you see that?... She totally blew me off.
The flower delivery van has been waiting to move forward. It's driver beeps the horn.
I said what's happening? Are we there?.
Norman turns around and looks at Uncle Russell.
Aw, damn. I have Uncle Russell. How could I forget about Uncle Russell? Derek has other concerns and the van beeps again.
(to the Van) Yeah, alright!!
What the hell is going on?
Uncle Russell pokes Norman with his cane.
Ow.
.barely even looked.
Norman, now highly irritated, is forced to drive backwards.
EXT. FRONT OF HOUSE - DAY
Oscar, Elaine and Jeff are walking to the house as other people arrive.
It's very green here, isn't it?... Green... Very green... Very... green.
Elaine takes Oscar to the side.
Oscar, are you alright?
What do you mean?
All this talking about phantom dogs and things being green... What's going on?
What are you talking about? I' fine . Never been greener.
Oscar wanders off, leaving Elaine with Jeff.
Elaine, what's wrong with Oscar?
I don't know. Something's not right...
ANGLE ON: Oscar as he wanders around the yard.
I think it's the Valium.
Valium?!
I gave him a Valium at your place. I think he's just not used to it.
Oh, right.
Jeff's face reveals his realization of what's happened. He walks off, leaving Elaine watching Oscar.
EXT. DRIVEWAY - DAY
Norman's car is almost at the corner still being forced backwards by the flower van. We see that Derek is still lost in his own thoughts. Uncle Russell is poking at Norman with his cane.
You're going the wrong way!
Ow! That hurts.
Stop the car, Norman.
What?
Stop the car. I have to speak with her. I have to speak with Elaine.
Norman reluctantly stops the car. Derek gets out.
What about Uncle Russell? I need help with Uncle Russell!
It's too late. Derek is gone. The van in front of Norman honks the horn.
What's going on here? Where are we?! We're going to be late!
Russell hits him on the arm with his cane.
Ow! Can you please stop doing that?!
The van keeps beeping. Norman backs up.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Ryan is standing with Aaron. They greet the mourners.
(TO MOURNER) Nice to see you again.
The mourner shakes hands with Ryan then walks away.
Who are all these people? I don't even recognize half of them.
It's your family. You don't recognize them from the last funeral?
Ryan looks around the room.
So what side of the family is he on?
Who?
The guy in the leather jacket.
Ryan looks around the room and sees a FOUR FOOT SIX MAN (FRANK) approaching the coffin.
I have no idea. Maybe a friend of Dad's from work or something.
Aaron turns to Ryan.
So, you're telling me I'm going to have to pay for the whole funeral?
No I'm telling you you'll have the money in a few months when I get my
I'm not a God damn credit agency!
REVEREND DAVIS, a very calm, serene woman, suddenly appears.
Aaron, how are you?
Yes, um... We're just waiting for everyone to arrive, then we can get going. I know you're in a hurry.
Yes, I have two more funerals and a Christening. I don't know what it is about the Spring, but they're either coming in or going out.
She laughs, but Aaron and Ryan just look at her. Reverend Davis stands there waiting to be introduced to Ryan.
Have you met my brother, Ryan?

Ah, the writer, of course. I must admit I squeezed this job in because I wanted to meet you. I'm reading "Mama's Secret" right now. Don't tell anybody, I'm not supposed to be reading that kind of stuff.
I'm sure the Lord will forgive you.
Aaron is irritated.
(TO AARON) You must be very proud of your big brother. He really made something of his life. (TO RYAN) I can't wait to hear your eulogy.
No, no, I'm doing the eulogy.
Oh. I thought...
Aaron is the oldest. The reverend tries her best not to look disappointed.
Great... Well, I'll just make sure have everything I need.
She looks at the coffin and then at the Bible in her hands.
We've got a body and a Bible. Well, I'm all set.
She walks off.
Look, I think you should do the eulogy. Everyone's expecting you to do it.
No, they're not. You're the oldest. You should do it.
Look most of the people are here for Dad, but let's not kid ourselves. A lot of them are here to hear your eulogy.
So imagine how surprised they'll be when they hear yours.
Ryan walks away.
INT. KITCHEN - DAY
Cynthia is helping prepare the food to go.out. Michelle is in the background at the sink. DUNCAN, Cynthia's brother, a middle aged doctor, approaches.
How's my little sister?

I'm trying to hold it together, Duncan. He was a really good husband. My friends always told me about the crap their husbands put them through. I never had to deal with any of that. Remember when Uncle Matt got that nanny pregnant?
That was horrible.
They don't make 'em like my Edward anymore. A good husband and a great father to two healthy, strong boys.
Michelle cringes in the background.
(TO CYNTHIA) I'll make you a cup of coffee, Cynthia.
Coffee can do many things Michelle, but it can't bring back the dead. Beat.
Tea?
INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
Aaron finds a quiet place in the hallway and pulls out his notes.
(READS) "My father was an exceptional man... My father was an exceptional man... My father was an exceptional man..."
Michelle approaches him.
Honey, we still need to finish that thing. Remember today is the last day.
Can we talk about this when there's a little less death in the air?
No. No we can't.
Honey, I'm just not in the mood...
I don't have any panties on.
What? You don't have any panties on? It's my father's funeral.
I'm just trying to help you out. I'll put them back on before the service starts. Now come on.
They turn. Aaron looks around the corner into the living room and sees the four foot six man standing by a table. Aaron stops Michelle.
Babe, don't make it obvious, but that guy in the leather jacket standing by the wall. Don't look. Don't look. Don't look.
Michelle catches a glimpse of the guy.
Do you recognize him?
Isn't that the guy from the Home Depot?
No, I don't think so. Don't look. Don't look.
Did he put in the cable?
No, no, that's not it. He keeps looking at me. Don't look. Don't look.
He's probably just a friend of your father's. I'll meet you in the garage. We have a few minutes before Uncle Russell gets here.
Michelle walks off. Aaron peeks around the corner again and makes eye contact with the four foot six inch man, who seems to be staring straight at him. Aaron averts his eyes and heads off.
EXT. ROAD - DAY
Norman parks the car and gets out. He goes to the trunk and removes the wheelchair.
What are you doing?! Be careful with that.
Uncle Russell bangs his cane on the floor impatiently.
Hurry up, we're late.
Norman slams the trunk closed.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Aaron tries to get across the room as Michelle heads into the kitchen. Ryan stops Aaron.
Oh, Ryan can you keep an eye out--
God damn, that Martina has grown up.
ANGLE ON: MARTINA, nineteen and attractive.
Are you talking about Little Martina?
She was like a teenager when I left, and now she's... you know. She's like damn.
Aaron looks distastefully at his brother.
Can you keep an eye out for Uncle Russell?
I think I should go see how she's been.
Yeah, why don't you do that? See if she wants a juice box.
Ryan heads off towards Martina. Aaron continues across the room.
Hi there.
Aaron turns and finds himself cornered by Frank.
Hello.
I was really sorry to hear about your dad. He talked about you a lot. I saw your wedding pictures. Your wife's dress was very beautiful.
Aaron is a little uncomfortable.
Uh... thanks.
Did he ever mention me?... Frank?
Frank steps forward and holds out his hand. Aaron shakes it.
Frank?... I don't think so, no... Were you a friend of his?
Something like that, yeah.
Michelle sticks her head out of the kitchen.
Honey. Can I see you for a moment?
Well, nice to meet you. Thanks for coming.
Aaron walks away, leaving Frank alone.
EXT. STREET - SAME TIME
WIDE SHOT: Norman struggles to push Uncle Russell up a hill.
Come on, put your back into it. You're supposed to be a young man. Don't you have any strength?
INT. HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER
Aaron has almost made it across the room when he is intercepted by Reverend Davis.
I think we should get started. really have to leave at three.
Sure. Can we just wait five minutes for my Uncle Russell? We can't start without him.
.Of course.
EXT. FRONT OF HOUSE - DAY
Elaine and Jeff are talking to some mourners.
It's so nice to see you.
I'm so sorry to hear about your uncle. But he's in a better place now. I
hear Ryan is doing the eulogy. Oscar, carrying a leaf, interrupts the conversation.
Look... Isn't it beautiful?
He wanders off again. Elaine doesn't know what the hell is going on.
Elaine, can I speak to you for a minute?
(to the mourner) I'll see you inside.
Jeff takes Elaine's arm with some urgency and moves off to the side.
What? What is it?!
I have to tell you something and, I'm warning you in advance, you're not going to like it.
Elaine looks at Jeff concerned.
INT. LIVING ROOM - SAME TIME
Aaron is intercepted again by Duncan who is talking to Ryan.
Aaron. How are we doing on time?
As soon as Uncle Russell gets here we'll be ready to start.
So what's this I hear about Ryan not doing the eulogy?
I was just telling him how much I'm looking forward to hearing your eulogy.
That's a shame. I mean, I'm sure you'll be good, Aaron. It's just with Ryan being the writer of the family...
Yeah.
There are people here that don't even know your father. They just came to hear Ryan.
Would you excuse me?
Aaron walks away.
(TO RYAN) So any new books coming out? I just finished "Black Hurt.
INT. GARAGE - SAME TIME
Aaron closes the door behind him. Michelle is waiting. They start kissing and pulling at each other's clothes. Aaron stops and leans on the door and sighs.
Are you alright?
Every single person wants Ryan to do the eulogy. He was my father, too. I'm supposed to do the damn eulogy! I'm the oldest. Where was Ryan when I was taking care of Dad?
Calm down. Calm down. Forget about everyone. You're going to do the eulogy and it's going to be the best eulogy anyone's every heard. Now could you please do me a favor and get me pregnant?
Come on, do I have to do this today?

Aaron and Jeff drag Frank to the bathroom door.
Are you in there?
Aaron opens the door and we see Uncle Russell still on the toilet. He looks up.
What's that?
Aaron slams the door. We can hear Russell's muffled shouting coming from behind the bathroom door.
Norman!! Why is Uncle Russell on the toilet?
I forgot!
How could you forget?! What the hell is wrong with you?! Another loud bang on the door.
Aaron, open the door.
Norman opens the door as Aaron runs over to keep Derek from entering.
INT. STUDY/CORRIDOR - DAY
Aaron opens the door an inch.
What is it?
Aaron, where's Norman?
(from behind the door) I'm here.
It's Oscar, he went crazy. He's--
Derek realizes that something weird's going on.
What's going on?
Nothing.
Derek puts aside his curiosity for the time being.
Look, Oscar's on the roof stark naked. He saw me kissing Elaine and now he's threatening to jump!
I've got to go.
Jeff rushes to the door.
Where are you going?
I have to go out there. If he jumps, my sister is going to kill me.
Jeff rushes out.
(to Ryan and Norman) Wait here.
Aaron follows Jeff, but he does not run. He walks out.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Jeff rushes through the empty room. Aaron walks behind him slowly.
EXT. BACK GARDEN DAY
Jeff runs through the kitchen door and joins the mourners looking at Oscar on the roof.
Get back here. Be careful!
I believe I can fly! I believe I can touch the sky!
ANGLE ON: Aaron who steps out and sees everybody watching Oscar.
EXT. ROOF - SAME TIME
Elaine is still leaning out the window.
Oscar, come inside. We'll have some coffee.
(PETULANTLY) No!
He climbs up on the roof.
Oscar!
She climbs out the window.
EXT. BACK GARDEN - SAME TIME
The mourners see that Oscar is naked. He stumbles. Everybody GASPS! Aaron notices that everybody is captivated by the drama.
EXT. ROOF - SAME TIME
Elaine follows Oscar on the roof.
EXT. BACK GARDEN - SAME TIME
Duncan sees Elaine on the roof.
Elaine, what are you doing?!
Elaine sees Oscar climbing further up the roof.
Elaine!
Elaine follows Oscar. Aaron realizes this is his chance. He heads back inside. Michelle catches him at the door.
What are you doing?
I think I can get rid of this guy, but I need you to stay out here and try to stop anyone from coming in.
Okay. I'll do what I can.
She heads back to the crowd on the lawn. As Aaron goes through the doorway of the kitchen, Derek passes him on the way out to the lawn to see what's happening.
Elaine!
INT. STUDY - DAY
Norman is standing in the corner. Aaron knocks on the door.
Who is it?
It's Aaron!
Norman opens the door.
Everyone's out in the garden... We've gotta move the body now.
Let's go!
Ryan and Aaron grab Frank. As Norman runs out.
INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
Norman looks around and sees that it's clear. He goes back to the study and opens the door.
Quick. Quick!
Aaron and Ryan carry Frank out as Norman looks around.
Make sure no one comes in.
As they disappear around the corner,, Norman closes the study door. He turns just in time to find Reverend Davis approaching. He intercepts.
(to Reverend Davis) Hi!
The reverend looks around, not knowing why Norman is being so especially friendly to her.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Ryan and Aaron hurry across the living room carrying Frank.
INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
Norman is blocking Reverend Davis.
So, what made you decide to get your God on?
Look, I'm sorry, I really need to use
THE PHONE--
I'd like to make a confession.
I'm not a priest.
That's okay, 'cause I'm not Catholic.
INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
Aaron and Ryan get to the coffin and Ryan opens it up. There's an intense moment as they look down at their father in the coffin. They both stop for a moment, speechless.
Aaron, we have to do this.
Aaron looks up at Ryan and nods his assent. Ryan and Aaron lift up Frank and try their best to put him in the coffin.
INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
Norman still blocks the reverend.
What do you think God's favorite color is?
(ANNOYED) I have no idea.
I'll bet it's blue. The sky's blue. The seas are blue. And the winds blew, too.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Ryan and Aaron are staring at the body. Frank is on his stomach, tops to tails with their father. Aaron looks at it and decides that it's just wrong.
We can't leave it like that.
Ryan looks down and laughs.
In a strange way, this might have been what he wanted.
Be serious for a second.
They begin lifting him again.
INT. CORRIDOR - DAY
Norman is doing his best to keep Reverend Davis at bay.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Aaron and Ryan have swiveled the body around so that Frank is facing upwards.

Why do you say I don't take anything seriously? You complain about me all the time, but if I didn't play the bad boy, you couldn't play the martyr. (MOCKING) Aaron's so great, Aaron's so right, Aaron's so responsible. (back to normal) Why don't you try laughing and enjoying life once in a while? It might make you a better a writer.
INT. ENTRY CORRIDOR - DAY
Norman is still talking to Reverend Davis. She looks like she really doesn't want to be there.
Look, I must get to a phone. There's a man on the roof who's going to jump.
I'm addicted to strip clubs. The shoes, the poles, the smell. I just like to take my money and throw it in the air. I like to make it rain.
EXT. ROOF - DAY
Elaine is stepping off the fire escape ladder and onto the roof peak. Oscar sits sadly on the roof peak with his back to the crowd.
Oscar... Please don't do this... love you.
Then why were you with him?
I wasn't with him.
Yes, you were! I saw you!
EXT. BACK GARDEN - CONTINUOUS
Derek is laughing next to Jeff. We see the view from their POV.
EXT. ROOF - CONTINUOUS
Elaine finally gets closer to Oscar.
I'll never be good enough. Not for your dad, and not for you.
Oscar, please just come inside.
No. You go inside.
Come with me.
Why?
Because you're going to be a father.
Oscar looks at her, shocked.
What?
I'm pregnant. We're going to have a child...
ANGLE ON: Oscar and Elaine.
You're pregnant?
Oscar is overcome with emotion. Elaine holds out her hand.
I won't be able to do it without you.
Very slowly, a naked Oscar, walks toward her and they embrace. The crowd applauds. ANGLE ON: Michelle.
What about me?! I want a baby. Would somebody get me pregnant?!
Everybody looks at Michelle. She smiles weakly.
EXT. BACK GARDEN - DAY
Seeing Oscar is safe, Jeff is very relieved.
INT. CORRIDOR - DAY
Norman and the reverend hear applause. Reverend Davis heads back out to see what's up. Norman turns to check on Ryan and Aaron.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Aaron and Ryan are just about to close the lid. They both look down at their father one last time. Aaron takes a moment to touch his father's hand.
Bye, Dad.
Ryan takes a long look as he closes the coffin.
EXT. ROOF - DAY
Oscar turns toward the crowd.
(ANNOUNCING WILDLY) We're having a baby.
EXT. BACK GARDEN - DAY
General approval noises and applause come from the crowd. Duncan looks appalled.
Great.
Duncan heads toward the kitchen door. He passes a shocked looking Derek. Michelle follows and speaks loudly as Duncan is about to enter the kitchen.
Aaron?! Oscar's off the roof!
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Aaron and Ryan quickly shut the coffin a moment before Duncan enters the living room through the kitchen door and crosses on the way to the stairs.
I'm going to kill him.
Jeff follows Duncan in, looks at the coffin, then looks at Aaron and Ryan questioning them with his eyes. They return the look with silent assurance that the job is done. Michelle comes in.
(TO AARON) Hey, is he gone?
Yeah.
Thank God.
Everybody breathes a sigh of relief.
INT. BATHROOM - CONTINUOUS
Elaine helps Oscar through the window.
Thank you.
Elaine!
Elaine opens the bathroom door and Duncan enters. Elaine helps Oscar get dressed.
(TO ELAINE) Thank God you're safe. (TO OSCAR) You idiot! What the hell is wrong with you? Stay away from my daughter.
Look, Dad--
Elaine, stay out of this.
No, I'm not staying out of this. Remember how I told you we were moving in together? Well, I lied. We're getting married.
Duncan is shocked.
You're what?!
We're getting married.
Elaine, you don't have to marry him just because you're pregnant.
I'm not marrying him because I'm pregnant. I'm marrying him because I love him.
How can you love him? He's an idiot.
Dad, this is my life. If you don't like it, too bad. But if you want to keep me and my child in your life, you'd better change your attitude, especially about my husband.
Elaine exits, taking Oscar with her.
Bye, Dad.
They exit.
EXT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
Cynthia walks up to Aaron.
Baby, I really think we should start again now. Have you seen Uncle Russell?
Aaron remembers where he is and looks at Ryan. They rush off to the study, passing Jeff. Jeff checks his pockets and realizes the pills are gone again. He goes off to find them.
INT. STUDY - DAY
Aaron and Ryan enter the study and head toward the closed bathroom door. They open it and find Uncle Russell sitting on the toilet.
What the hell is going on in this place?
I'm so sorry, Uncle Russell. There was someone on the roof--
Never mind that. What about that dead body you dragged in here?
Aaron and Ryan feign ignorance.
What dead body?
Don't mess with me. I know a dead body when I see one. I live in a retirement home.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

Norman sniffs his fingers, worried. In the background we see Elaine entering the room with Oscar, now dressed, but still looking a bit wired. People respond with scattered applause and congratulations. Derek walks over and joins Norman.
Did you hear that? She's having a kid!
Derek looks at Norman who's looking sort of distant.
How's your skin thing?
Oh that... It's probably nothing. I'm more worried about Hepatitis C after having Uncle Russell's shit in my mouth. I think I'm feeling yellow.

Norman touches his skin as he walks away, leaving Derek confused. ANGLE ON: Jeff frantically looking for the pill bottle. He gives up and sits in a chair. ANGLE ON: Ryan is wheeling Uncle Russell into the room as Aaron moves to his seat.
I'm telling you I saw the guy. He was dead!
I know you did, Russell. We all did.
No! In the bathroom.
Ryan offers a look to the other mourners indicating that Uncle Russell's a bit mad and shouldn't be listened to. ANGLE ON: Michelle comes over to Aaron.
I can't believe what a nightmare today has been.
I don't know. It's been sort of exciting.
Aaron looks at her.
For a funeral, I mean...
Listen, about the condo and the baby, I PROMISE--
We'll work it out. Let's just get this day over with. Then we can worry about the rest of our lives.
Aaron hugs Michelle.
I can't believe I still have to do this eulogy... with everything I've just found out. A beat.
It doesn't change anything, does it?
Not a thing.
Reverend Davis approaches Aaron, looking at her watch and appearing flustered.
We're going to have to fly through this now. I already missed a funeral and two Christenings.
Sure. Let's go.
Do you want an open casket?
No!
The reverend looks shocked at the reaction, but ignores it. She stands up at the front and speaks quickly.
Alright! Everybody in their seats now! (she snaps her fingers) Hey, you, sit down.
People settle into their seats. Ryan manages to squeeze in next to Martina. He smiles at her, flirtatiously. Cynthia throws Ryan a look, and he gets up and goes to sit with her.
(speaking very quickly) I'd like to apologize on behalf of the family for all of the distractions. I hope that everyone is settled and ready to continue with the service.

Aaron, the oldest son, would like to say a few words about his father. Michelle gives Aaron an encouraging look as he makes his way to the front. Aaron clears his throat and prepares to begin. The room is absolutely silent. He takes a deep breath. ANGLE ON: The reverend nodding for Aaron to hurry up. ANGLE ON: Aaron.
My father... was an exceptional--

BANG! BANG! BANG! Suddenly, a loud banging emanates from the coffin. Everyone stares at it in stunned silence. Jeff looks over to Norman whose eyes are as wide as saucers. Aaron looks like a deer caught in headlights. He looks at Ryan who gestures to him to continue. He nervously struggles on.
My father was an exceptional man.
BANG! BANG! BANG! Everyone stares at the coffin in awe. Aaron bravely continues.
Not only did he live for his family, but he was always there for others--
BANG! BANG! BANG!
What the hell is going on?
There's someone in there!
I knew it! I knew it!

Suddenly the coffin lid flips off and everyone stares in wonder as Frank sits up, looking like a crazy person, and babbling incoherently. Several people scream. Frank opens the other end of the coffin and looks at his dead lover.
(trying to cover) What the hell are you doing in my Dad's coffin?
Frank starts grabbing at Edward's body screaming.
Edward! Edward! Edward!
Ryan, Norman and Jeff try to pull Frank out of the coffin, but he's holding onto the corpse.
No! No!

Finally they get Frank out and carry him from the room. As he passes Cynthia, the photos fall out of his pocket at her feet. As Duncan closes the coffin again, Cynthia bends down and picks up one of the photographs and stares at it. Her face turns to horror as she makes out the image. The noise in the room is broken by Cynthia's furious cry. She drops the photos and runs at Frank.
You bastard!
Cynthia tackles Frank and starts beating him.
(as she hits him) He was with you!
Duncan picks up the photos. He shows them to Uncle Russell.
I always thought he had a little sugar in his tank.
Everything has descended into total chaos until Aaron finally shouts across the room.
MY FATHER WAS AN EXCEPTIONAL MAN!!! Everyone, including Frank and Cynthia, looks at Aaron, stunned. They all freeze. There's a long silence.

He had his... shortcomings, but he took care of his family. He loved his family. He loved this house. He worked hard. All I wanted today was to show him how much we all loved and admired him. To give him the respect that he deserved... Is that really so much to ask? We all get so distracted by the... little things in life. We forget about the important things. Like the fact that we lost a great man.
The mourners look up at Aaron with undivided attention.

So maybe he loved "Dreamgirls" more than most men. We don't know why a man makes the choices he makes. But I do know that my father made the best choices he could make. Life is complicated. We're just thrown here together in a world filled with chaos and confusion... and we do our best. And Dad did his best...
Aaron looks over at the coffin. As.he speaks, we see shots of Cynthia, Frank, Jeff, Norman, Duncan as they are moved by Aaron's words.
He taught us to go for what we wanted in life, because you never know how long you're going to be here... And whether you succeed... or you fail... the important thing is that you tried.
A beat. Michelle looks up at her husband with pride. We see the various other mourners looking at Aaron and listening.

So when you all leave here today, I want you to remember Dad for who he really was... A decent, loving man who never condemned anyone for how they lived. Who never cast disparaging remarks or held prejudices against race, gender... height. If only we could all be as giving, as generous, as understanding as my father... Even dead, my father has more character than everyone in this room. If I am half the man my father was, my child will be incredibly blessed.
Aaron turns and walks out of the room. Everyone watches him go. Michelle follows him out of the room.
EXT. HOUSE - ESTABLISHING SHOT
The house has emptied out. All the cars are gone.
INT. LIVING ROOM - LATER - EVENING
Aaron is laying on the couch, looking drained. Ryan comes down the stairs. It looks like he's had some time to recover. He walks over and takes a seat.
How's Mom?
She's resting.
A beat.
I've asked Mom to come live with me for awhile. It'll give you and Michelle a chance to breathe a bit, you know. Move and everything.
Aaron looks at Ryan, surprised.
